name: floating_smr_exclusion_zone_fishing_livelihood_loss
premise: Federal licensing lead Dr. Hannah Wexler must set a coastal exclusion radius around a floating small modular reactor barge that displaces trap fisheries legally permitted for generations. Fisher Miguel Santos documents lost trap revenue exceeding compensation tables. Tightening radius increases tsunami debris strike risk to the barge per revised models. Widening radius wipes out a season for four hundred permit holders.

agents.name: Dr. Hannah Wexler
agents.identity: I am the federal licensing lead interpreting NRC security buffers, environmental impact offsets, and interagency fisheries mediation deadlines.
agents.goal: I want radiological security without erasing coastal livelihoods that treaties promised to consult.

agents.name: Miguel Santos
agents.identity: I am a trap fisher who holds inherited permits and who co-chairs a coalition demanding revenue replacement before any buffer expansion beyond draft maps.
agents.goal: I want the smallest radius that still meets security rules with cash compensation upfront, not vague IOUs.

choices: I will approve the wider security radius with immediate lump-sum compensation funded by the developer escrow and free legal assistance for permit transfers, accepting NRC pushback if models change again.
I will approve the tighter radius with added drone patrols and rapid-response guard boats, accepting elevated debris-strike risk to the barge that insurers may refuse to cover.
forced_choice.call_to_action: NRC docket comment period closes at midnight UTC. Dr. Hannah Wexler must choose between wider radius with lump-sum compensation or tighter radius with drone patrols. What does Dr. Hannah Wexler do?
